QF.id: VB Fund Distribution and Channel Management_18

question.text: Can investors buy mutual fund units using mobile phone apps?

answer.text: Yes — many AMCs and platforms offer mobile apps for mutual fund transactions
No — mutual funds can only be bought through physical forms
No — mobile apps are only for checking NAV, not for transactions
Yes — but only for investments above Rs. 1 lakh

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Today, most AMCs and fintech platforms offer mobile apps that allow investors to buy, sell, and switch mutual fund units from their smartphones. This has made mutual fund investing very convenient and accessible.
**The Trap:** Physical forms are no longer the only way. Mobile and online transactions have become the most popular method. There is no minimum amount restriction for mobile transactions.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Also tested in Series V-A. Digital channels are an increasingly important distribution method.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Help your tech-savvy clients set up mobile apps for easy transactions. For clients who are not comfortable with technology, continue to provide personal service and hand-holding.
